response: The magazine created first was **Macworld**. It was founded in 1984 as a print magazine by David Bunnell (publisher) and Andrew Fluegelman (editor), and it was the oldest Macintosh magazine still in publication until September 10, 2014, when it discontinued its print edition. In contrast, **Soaps In Depth** was created in 1997 by Bauer Publications, giving American soap opera viewers more variety in their soap-related magazine purchases.
